I wonder what kind of societal, economic, or cultural factors contributed to this period of decline. I also find it intriguing that the Greeks were able to develop their alphabet and create foundational works of literature like the Iliad and the Odyssey despite this period of darkness.

I'm fascinated by the concept of the Polis. The idea that the Polis emerged as a state form around 800-500 v. Chr. and became a fundamental aspect of ancient Greek civilization resonates with me. The fact that it was a system of governance that was both decentralized and yet still maintained a sense of unity among the city-states is intriguing. I'd love to learn more about how this system functioned and how it influenced the development of Western civilization.
